title: Apple Ferment Crunch
description: A buttery, crispy brunch dish featuring apple juice and a fermented component in a dairy-free-friendly Moroccan-inspired recipe.

ingredients.name: apples
ingredients.notes: use firm apples like Granny Smith or Honeycrisp

ingredients.name: apple cider vinegar
ingredients.notes: organic and unfiltered for best flavor

ingredients.name: almond flour or almond meal
ingredients.notes: ensure it is gluten-free if necessary

ingredients.name: coconut oil
ingredients.notes: for a non-dairy alternative, use coconut oil

ingredients.name: all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: can be swapped for almond flour in the final mix if needed

instructions: Peel and core the apples. Chop them into chunks about an inch square.
In a large bowl, combine apple cider vinegar with chopped apples, maple syrup, and coconut oil.
Mix well to ensure all apple pieces are evenly coated.
Add salt and stir until fully incorporated.
Transfer mixture onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Flatten the mixture into a thin layer using your hands or a spatula.
Preheat oven to 200°C (392°F). Bake for about 15-20 minutes, or until edges begin to turn golden brown and it becomes crisp.
Let it cool slightly before slicing into small pieces. Serve as a topping on yogurt bowls or use in breakfast sandwiches.
